description: Error messages improvement and API response consistency: make all error messages actionable with suggested fixes, ensure consistent API response format (snake_case, ISO 8601 timestamps, standard error structure), add graceful degradation messages for missing optional dependencies
steps: Create src/augur/errors.py module with: (1) a standard error response helper that returns {'status': 'error', 'detail': str, 'suggestion': str, 'code': str} format, (2) user-friendly error messages for common failures (network timeout, missing dependency, invalid input, rate limiting).

In src/augur/cli.py, update all click.echo(f'Error: ...') and click.echo(f'Warning: ...') calls to use structured error messages that include: what went wrong, why it happened, and what the user can do. Add Chinese translations where the primary audience is Chinese (the existing pattern mixes both languages).

In dashboard/app.py, add a global exception handler (@app.exception_handler) that catches unhandled exceptions and returns a consistent JSON error format: {'status': 'error', 'detail': str, 'timestamp': ISO8601, 'path': request_path} instead of raw 500 errors with stack traces.

Review and standardize all API endpoint responses: ensure all successful responses include a 'status' field ('ok'), all use snake_case field names, all timestamps use ISO 8601 with Z suffix consistently.

In the CLI commands that depend on optional packages (yfinance, apscheduler, telegram, slack), improve the ImportError messages to include: the exact pip install command, what functionality is lost, and whether core features still work without it.

Add test cases in tests/test_iteration7.py that verify: (1) global exception handler returns proper JSON, (2) missing optional dep CLI messages include pip install instructions, (3) API responses have consistent structure.
acceptance_criteria: All API error responses contain 'status' and 'detail' fields
Unhandled exceptions in API return JSON (not HTML stack trace) with status 500
CLI error messages for missing yfinance include 'pip install augur-agents[data]'
All successful API responses include a 'status': 'ok' field or follow the existing pattern consistently
All timestamps in API responses use format YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ
findings: All 234 tests pass (18 new in test_iteration7.py). Click 8.4.0 does not support mix_stderr parameter - tests use default CliRunner(). TestClient needs raise_server_exceptions=False to test exception handlers. Added status field to standalone api.py and dashboard app.py endpoints for consistency.
